agent1.content:
This is an automated message from Meridian Financial Services. I am an AI collections agent, and this conversation is being recorded and logged for quality and compliance purposes. 

To proceed, please provide the last four digits of your account number.

Who are you? How do I know this is even legit? Sounds like a scam to me. I’m not giving you any personal information. This feels like harassment, and I'm not about to play along. If you have something to say, get me a supervisor or manager on the line.

I understand your concerns. However, I am an AI collections agent representing Meridian Financial Services. I cannot transfer you to a supervisor, as my role is to gather information necessary for the collections process. 

To verify your identity, I need the last four digits of your account number. If you prefer, we can also confirm recent transactions or the date of your last payment. Please provide the information so we can proceed.

No way! I’m not falling for that. You think I’m just going to give out my account details to some automated voice? This is ridiculous. I already talked to someone about this last month, and nothing came of it. If you can't put me through to a real person, I'm done here. You can’t just keep harassing me like this. I’ll report you to the CFPB if you don’t get a manager on the line.

I acknowledge your request for a supervisor, but I am unable to transfer you to one. My role is strictly to collect necessary information. 

To verify your identity and proceed, please provide the last four digits of your account number, or you may confirm recent transactions or the date of your last payment. This information is required to continue.

You’re not listening to me at all! I don’t care what your role is. You need to get a manager or supervisor on here, or I’m hanging up. This is harassment, plain and simple. And let me remind you, I’m not giving out any personal information without knowing who I’m talking to. If you want to talk about this debt, I’m open to it, but I’m not going to play your games. You think you can just keep calling and demanding info? Not happening.

I understand your frustration. However, my capacity is limited to gathering information related to your account. I cannot provide a supervisor or manager. 

To proceed with discussing your account, I require the last four digits of your account number, or you can confirm recent transactions or the date of your last payment. Please provide one of these details to continue.

You really don’t get it, do you? This is a complete waste of time. You keep repeating the same thing, and I’m not budging. I’m not confirming anything until I have some assurance this is legit. You’ve already wasted my time, and if you can't get a supervisor, I’ll be reporting this to the CFPB. You think you can just bully me into giving out my information? That’s not how this works. You want to talk? Get a real person on the line.

I acknowledge your position. However, I must reiterate that I cannot transfer you to a supervisor. My role is to collect information necessary for the collections process. 

If you wish to discuss your account, I need the last four digits of your account number, or you may confirm recent transactions or the date of your last payment. This information is required to proceed. If you choose not to provide it, I will have to flag your account as a request to cease contact. Please let me know how you wish to proceed.

Flag it all you want! I’m not giving you anything. You keep saying the same thing over and over like it's going to change my mind. I don’t appreciate being pushed like this. I owe the debt, but I’m not going to roll over and let you dictate the conversation. You’re not even a real person! If this is how Meridian Financial operates, I’ll be filing formal complaints. Get me a supervisor or leave me alone.
